citations.narrative:
The facility does not maintain complete medical records for adult dogs. The facility states that the adult dogs were up to
date on Distemper, Parvovirus, and Rabies vaccines, but they could not provide documentation for most vaccines
administered. Failure to maintain adequate medical records may interfere with the ability of APHIS officials to determine if
the animals are receiving adequate veterinary care. Complete medical records must be maintained and include the
names of all vaccines and treatments administered and the dates of administration. Correct by documenting all treatments
given and ensure that medical records are complete and contain the required information. Correct by 26-MAR-2026.

The facility was unable to provide documentation of heartworm or fecal tests performed. The facility stated that the tests
are performed in accordance with the schedule in the Program of Veterinary Care but they could not provide the test
results. Failure to document testing results may interfere with the ability of APHIS officials to determine if the animals are
receiving adequate veterinary care. Medical records must be maintained and include the dates and findings/results of all
screening, routine, or other required or recommended test or examination. Correct by documenting the results of all
testing and ensure that medical records are complete and contain the required information. Correct by 26-MAR-2026.
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with a Facility Representative.
n

narrative:
3.13(b)(3) 
   
Veterinary care for dogs. 
The facility does not maintain complete medical records for adult dogs. The facility states that the adult dogs were up to 
date on Distemper, Parvovirus, and Rabies vaccines, but they could not provide documentation for most vaccines 
administered. Failure to maintain adequate medical records may interfere with the ability of APHIS officials to determine if 
the animals are receiving adequate veterinary care. Complete medical records must be maintained and include the 
names of all vaccines and treatments administered and the dates of administration. Correct by documenting all treatments 
given and ensure that medical records are complete and contain the required information. Correct by 26-MAR-2026. 
3.13(b)(4) 
   
Veterinary care for dogs. 
The facility was unable to provide documentation of heartworm or fecal tests performed. The facility stated that the tests 
are performed in accordance with the schedule in the Program of Veterinary Care but they could not provide the test 
results. Failure to document testing results may interfere with the ability of APHIS officials to determine if the animals are 
receiving adequate veterinary care. Medical records must be maintained and include the dates and findings/results of all 
screening, routine, or other required or recommended test or examination. Correct by documenting the results of all 
testing and ensure that medical records are complete and contain the required information. Correct by 26-MAR-2026. 
 
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with a Facility Representative.
